title: dianthus laricifolius subsp. caespitosifolius
intro: Dianthus laricifolius subsp. caespitosifolius, also known as the alpine pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its needle-like leaves and pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Dianthus laricifolius subsp. caespitosifolius include its ability to calm the nervous system, reduce stress, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, such as anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems.
